meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master Real-World Apps for Students
content.seo_description: Break through your coding plateau. Learn to build professional-grade SaaS applications using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building tutorials, not products. A SaaS starter kit forces you to handle authentication, database schemas, and billing logic—the 'boring' infrastructure that defines professional software. By automating this setup with Antigravity and Claude Code, you transition from a coder to a product-focused AI Builder.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' app, yet the hollow feeling remains. You have mastered the syntax, but you are trapped in a cycle of trivial projects that never see a real user. The gap between a student project and a production-ready SaaS is not more code; it is architectural discipline.

Today, we bridge that gap. By leveraging a high-velocity SaaS starter kit combined with the orchestration power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you will stop reinventing the wheel. We are moving beyond the classroom to build systems that scale, secure data, and solve actual problems.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation using a clean starter kit architecture to prevent spaghetti code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS starter kit architecture using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include clear separation between API routes, database hooks, and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'Setup base project structure with Next.js and Supabase'

steps.title: Implement Multi-Tenant Authentication
steps.description: Secure your app by setting up role-based access control, ensuring user data is isolated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware for Next.js that handles user roles and organization-level data isolation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Auth.js middleware for multi-tenant access control'

steps.title: Database Schema Modeling
steps.description: Design a relational database schema that supports scalable user growth and subscription tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform including tables for users, organizations, subscriptions, and usage logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file schema.prisma --task 'Define relations for User, Organization, and Subscription models'

steps.title: Stripe Integration & Webhooks
steps.description: Connect your payment gateway to handle subscription life cycles and automated billing events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the Supabase users table.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Stripe webhook endpoint to update subscription status in DB'

steps.title: Deployment & CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the delivery of your code to production to ensure consistent updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Vercel deployment pipeline via GitHub Actions'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema before writing a single line of frontend logic to avoid refactoring hell.
Use environment variables for everything; never hardcode a secret, even in development.
Implement 'feature flags' early to test new functionality with a subset of users without breaking the main build.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first; edge cases are important, but shipping a functional MVP is your priority.
Audit your dependencies regularly; a bloated node_modules folder is the silent killer of build speeds.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is deployed, focus on adding one unique value-add feature that solves a specific user pain point. Then, share your build in the community to gather feedback and refine your product.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to stop building toys and start building businesses. The industry is waiting for engineers who can execute from architecture to deployment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
